name: Bulk_Mech

infobox.Systems: Ricochet Rocket(Brute)EffectBounce a projectile off one target to hit another (pushes each target).(1 damage)Upgrade I+1 Damage ()+1 damage to both targets.Upgrade IIAlly Immune ()Friendly Units will not take damage from this attack.

sections.Team: The mech is available as part of theArachnophilessquad.
sections.Strategy:
The Bulk Mech is designed to spread damage and displace multiple targets with its weapon, both from pushing them away and from bumping enemies into each other. Getting kills with Ricochet Rocket is less important in the Arachnophiles squad, as theArachnoid Mechand its spawned arachnoids are well-suited to finishing off low health enemies. However, the Bulk Mech is also prone to committing friendly fire – particularly in tight spaces – as the ricochet can potentially prevent it from pushing enemies safely without damaging a nearby building.
Ricochet Rocket can target two units or structures; the first target is pushed directly away from the Bulk Mech, while the second target must be perpendicular to the first target, and will be pushed laterally. Lateral pushes or shots that hit two targets at once can be hard to set up; in the Arachnophiles squad, theSlide Mechcan help position Vek targets to let the Bulk Mech accomplish this.Mountainscan also be used as the first target, which is recommended to do so in congested maps with little room to move through tight spaces.
Since the Bulk Mech must bounce the rocket projectile after hitting one target, it needs clear lines of fire in order to avoid damaging Grid Buildings. Upgrading the mobility of the Bulk Mech early on can help it here, as it increases its potential lines of fire and reduces the likelihood of the ricochet buildings. Other player Mechs can be used for the necessary bounce trajectory; thus, theAlly Immuneupgrade is also a good early unlock, so ally Mechs, spawned Arachnoids, and other mission objective units (for e.g. theSupply Train) can be used freely as bounce targets.
If using the Bulk Mech under aCustom Squad, it pairs best with Mechs that can set up ricochets for it to maximize its potential targets; as an example, theJudo Mechand can throw an enemy in between buildings away from them, a situation in which the Bulk Mech could struggle to do so safely without hitting one of the buildings with the bounced projectile. The Judo Mech also has armor, which enables the Ricochet Rocket from bouncing freely from the Judo Mech without damaging it, and depending less on the Ally Immune upgrade.

Strategy

The Bulk Mech is designed to spread damage and displace multiple targets with its weapon, both from pushing them away and from bumping enemies into each other. Getting kills with Ricochet Rocket is less important in the Arachnophiles squad, as the
Arachnoid Mech
and its spawned arachnoids are well-suited to finishing off low health enemies. However, the Bulk Mech is also prone to committing friendly fire – particularly in tight spaces – as the ricochet can potentially prevent it from pushing enemies safely without damaging a nearby building.
Ricochet Rocket can target two units or structures; the first target is pushed directly away from the Bulk Mech, while the second target must be perpendicular to the first target, and will be pushed laterally. Lateral pushes or shots that hit two targets at once can be hard to set up; in the Arachnophiles squad, the
Slide Mech
can help position Vek targets to let the Bulk Mech accomplish this.
Mountains
can also be used as the first target, which is recommended to do so in congested maps with little room to move through tight spaces.
Since the Bulk Mech must bounce the rocket projectile after hitting one target, it needs clear lines of fire in order to avoid damaging Grid Buildings. Upgrading the mobility of the Bulk Mech early on can help it here, as it increases its potential lines of fire and reduces the likelihood of the ricochet buildings. Other player Mechs can be used for the necessary bounce trajectory; thus, the
Ally Immune
upgrade is also a good early unlock, so ally Mechs, spawned Arachnoids, and other mission objective units (for e.g. the
Supply Train
) can be used freely as bounce targets.
If using the Bulk Mech under a
Custom Squad
, it pairs best with Mechs that can set up ricochets for it to maximize its potential targets; as an example, the
Judo Mech
and can throw an enemy in between buildings away from them, a situation in which the Bulk Mech could struggle to do so safely without hitting one of the buildings with the bounced projectile. The Judo Mech also has armor, which enables the Ricochet Rocket from bouncing freely from the Judo Mech without damaging it, and depending less on the Ally Immune upgrade.
